name: Consul Autopilot Unhealthy
description: Alert when consul.autopilot.healthy drops to 0. Indicates that one or more Consul servers have become unhealthy and the cluster has lost its safety margin.

name: Consul Leadership Instability - Elections Detected
description: Alert when Raft elections are observed (consul.raft.state.candidate > 0). Frequent elections indicate network issues or resource exhaustion on Consul servers. A healthy cluster should have 0 elections in steady state.

name: Consul Raft Leader Last Contact Elevated
description: Alert when consul.raft.leader.lastContact exceeds 200 ms. A value above 200 ms indicates the Raft heartbeat channel is under stress and leadership flapping may follow.

name: Consul Raft Commit Time Elevated (p99)
description: Alert when consul.raft.commitTime p99 exceeds 50 ms. Elevated commit times indicate write pressure on the Consul cluster and can lead to timeouts for API clients.

name: Consul RPC Rate Limiting Active
description: Alert when consul.client.rpc.exceeded > 0. RPC rate limiting means a Consul client is being throttled by the server, which can delay service registrations, health check updates, and catalog queries.

name: Consul RPC Failures Detected
description: Alert when consul.client.rpc.failed > 0. RPC failures indicate that client agents cannot reach server agents, which will degrade service discovery and health check reporting.

name: Consul Raft Main Thread Saturation High
description: Alert when consul.raft.thread.main.saturation exceeds 50%. High saturation means the Consul server is approaching its write capacity limit and may become unstable under additional load.

name: Consul Raft FSM Thread Saturation High
description: Alert when consul.raft.thread.fsm.saturation exceeds 50%. High FSM saturation indicates the state machine is unable to keep pace with incoming commits, increasing the risk of replication lag.

name: Consul Root CA Expiry Warning (30 days)
description: Warning when consul.mesh.active_root_ca.expiry drops below 2592000 seconds (30 days). The root CA is NOT automatically rotated by Consul and requires explicit operator action. Plan rotation well in advance.

name: Consul Root CA Expiry Critical (7 days)
description: Critical alert when consul.mesh.active_root_ca.expiry drops below 604800 seconds (7 days). The service mesh will fail entirely when the root CA expires. Immediate operator action required.

name: Consul Signing CA Expiry Warning (24 hours)
description: Warning when consul.mesh.active_signing_ca.expiry drops below 86400 seconds (24 hours). Consul should rotate the signing CA automatically, but check server logs for CA rotation failures if this alert fires.

name: Consul Agent TLS Certificate Expiry Warning (7 days)
description: Warning when consul.agent.tls.cert.expiry drops below 604800 seconds (7 days). The agent TLS certificate is used for inter-agent communication. Expiry will cause agents to reject connections from one another.

name: Consul Enterprise License Expiry Warning (30 days)
description: Warning when consul.system.licenseExpiration drops below 720 hours (30 days). Enterprise features begin degrading when the license expires. Open a HashiCorp support ticket to renew.

name: Consul Enterprise License Expiry Critical (7 days)
description: Critical alert when consul.system.licenseExpiration drops below 168 hours (7 days). Namespace operations and other Enterprise features will stop working upon expiry.

name: Consul Dataplane Server Disconnected
description: Critical alert when consul_dataplane.connected = 0. The consul-dataplane sidecar has lost its connection to all Consul servers. Envoy will stop receiving xDS updates and mTLS certificates cannot be refreshed.

name: Consul Dataplane Envoy xDS Disconnected
description: Warning when consul_dataplane.envoy_connected = 0. Envoy is not connected to consul-dataplane and is not receiving xDS configuration updates. Service routing and mTLS may be stale.

name: Service Upstream Error Rate High (Warning)
description: Warning when 5xx responses exceed 1% of total upstream requests to any service cluster over 5 minutes. Indicates a degraded upstream service — check logs and traces for the affected cluster.

name: Service Upstream Error Rate Critical
description: Critical alert when 5xx responses exceed 5% of total upstream requests to any service cluster over 5 minutes. Service is significantly degraded and likely impacting end users.

name: Service Upstream p99 Latency High (Warning)
description: Warning when p99 upstream request latency exceeds 500ms over 5 minutes. A sustained high p99 indicates a struggling upstream service or network congestion on the service mesh path.

name: Service Upstream p99 Latency Critical
description: Critical alert when p99 upstream request latency exceeds 2000ms over 5 minutes. Service is likely breaching SLOs and users are experiencing significant degradation.

name: Upstream Service Has No Healthy Endpoints
description: Critical alert when upstream_cx_none_healthy > 0 for any service cluster. Envoy has no healthy endpoints to route requests to — all instances of the target service are down or failing health checks. All traffic to this service will fail immediately.

name: Upstream Circuit Breaker Active
description: Warning when upstream_cx_overflow or upstream_rq_pending_overflow > 0. Envoy's circuit breaker is shedding load to prevent cascade failure. The upstream service is overloaded or the connection pool limits are misconfigured.

name: Upstream Request Queue Overflow
description: Warning when upstream_rq_pending_overflow > 0. Requests are being dropped before they reach the upstream because the pending request queue is full. Indicates the upstream cannot keep up with the request rate.

name: Upstream Request Timeout Rate Elevated
description: Warning when upstream request timeouts exceed 0 sustained over 5 minutes. Timeouts indicate the upstream service is slow to respond — check for resource exhaustion, GC pauses, or downstream dependency issues on the target service.
